us_equities.prose: Stocks rallied broadly after the United States and Iran signed an interim peace deal, with the agreement easing geopolitical tensions and lifting sentiment across risk assets.

cited_sources.title: U.S. Stocks Poised to Close Week on Strong Note

rates_and_dollar.prose: Treasury yields and the dollar rose after the Federal Reserve held rates unchanged in a unanimous decision at Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chairman, while market participants began pricing growing odds of a rate hike later in the year.

cited_sources.title: Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise on First Fed Move Under Warsh

commodities.prose: Oil fell sharply after President Trump signed a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with Saudi tankers immediately resuming transit and the IEA warning that a lasting resolution could trigger a major supply overhang, while gold eased as geopolitical fear receded.

cited_sources.title: Oil Prices Fall to Lowest Since War Began After Trump Signs Iran Deal

volatility_breadth.prose: Fear gauges eased as the US-Iran peace deal reduced geopolitical uncertainty and oil supply fears, pulling hedging demand lower.

cited_sources.title: Gas prices fall below $4 per gallon as oil supply fears ease after Iran deal
